svg.text: CASE STUDY — SOCIAL ENGINEERING & CROSS-CHAIN LAUNDERING
Case AML-IR-2026-005 · Kraken User · $18.2M stolen · THORChain laundering · March 31, 2026 · ZachXBT alert
Each hop: new chain + new address type
a regulated VASP or instant exchange
✓ Apply EDD on large BTC deposits

g.text: • Sudden full-balance withdrawal from long-dormant account — ACAMS 3.1.2
• New device/IP + large outflow in same session — ACAMS 6.2.1 · FATF VA §4.2
• Unhosted wallet destination with no prior relationship — FATF R.16
• Cross-chain bridge activity (THORChain) within hours of account access — FATF VA §5.3
• Fan-out to multiple BTC addresses post-bridge — ACAMS 4.3.3
• SafePal / non-custodial wallet relay hop — ACAMS 5.1.1
• Temporal proximity to ZachXBT / CertiK public alert — automatic EDD trigger
Frameworks: ACAMS 6.2.1 · FATF VA §4.2/5.3 · BSA · GENIUS Act 2025 · EU 6AMLD Art.2
Behavioral AML required alongside on-chain analytics — SE attacks begin before any transaction
1. Session anomaly detection: new device/IP + full balance withdrawal = mandatory step-up auth
2. Withdrawal velocity limits + delay window (30 min minimum) on large unhosted wallet transfers
3. Pre-execution screening of ALL unhosted wallet destinations vs. real-time threat intelligence
4. Anti-SE callback: proactive outbound call to registered number for large withdrawals (independent of inbound contact)
5. Integrate TRM Beacon Network / Chainalysis KYT for real-time flagged address sharing
6. Subscribe to ZachXBT and CertiK public intelligence feeds — 45-minute window is the intervention opportunity
7. Configure cross-chain bridge inflow monitoring: flag BTC deposits with THORChain history within 24h lookback
Key insight: The 45-minute window between first movement and public alert = the compliance intervention window
ACAMS CAMS 10th Ed. · FATF VA Guidance 2021 · BSA/FinCEN SAR · GENIUS Act 2025 · EU 6AMLD

svg.text: Author: Phelipe Agnelli — AML & Blockchain Forensics · Sources: ZachXBT · CertiK March 2026 Report · BeInCrypto · CoinDesk · Chainalysis 2026 Crypto Crime Report · ACAMS · FATF
